description: Milestone 9: Operational hardening & governance readiness for an official party tool—security reviews, privacy documentation, reliability, accessibility, and release process.

userStories.title: Security hardening pass (auth + storage + networking)
userStories.description: Review and tighten security across auth, keychain, logging, and network layers.
acceptanceCriteria: Audit and remove any accidental sensitive logging (tokens, message bodies, identifiers)
Add centralized redaction utilities and apply them across the codebase
Document security assumptions and mitigations in Docs/THREAT_MODEL.md
Context7 consulted for iOS security logging guidance; summary added to progress.txt

userStories.title: Privacy controls and transparency improvements
userStories.description: Ensure users can control what data is shown and how notifications behave.
acceptanceCriteria: Settings clearly explain data usage and permissions (notifications, profile visibility where applicable)
Add an in-app Privacy page summarizing key points
No analytics/tracking introduced
Context7 consulted for privacy UI patterns and Apple guidelines; summary added to progress.txt

userStories.title: Accessibility pass for core flows
userStories.description: Improve accessibility for the main tabs and critical flows.
acceptanceCriteria: VoiceOver labels for primary controls and lists
Dynamic Type support for core screens
Contrast issues identified and corrected
Context7 consulted for SwiftUI accessibility best practices; summary added to progress.txt

userStories.title: Resilience and offline/poor-network behavior
userStories.description: Make behavior predictable under poor connectivity and API failures.
acceptanceCriteria: Consistent loading/error/empty states across Forum, Messages, Todos
Retry behavior is bounded (no infinite loops); documented in Docs/DECISIONS.md
Optional caching policy documented and implemented if low-risk
Context7 consulted for URLSession caching and network resilience patterns; summary added to progress.txt

userStories.title: Release readiness checklist and basic CI documentation
userStories.description: Define a minimal, repeatable release process suitable for an official tool.
acceptanceCriteria: Docs/RELEASE_CHECKLIST.md exists with steps for versioning, signing, and privacy review
Docs/CI_NOTES.md documents the xcodebuild commands used by Ralph and local dev
No secrets committed; sensitive config via env/xcconfig documented
Context7 consulted for modern Xcode build/test automation guidance; summary added to progress.txt
